questions.question: What are the three essential dimensions of food security?
questions.explanation: Food security means availability of food for all, accessibility within reach, and affordability to buy safe and nutritious food.

questions.question: When is a person considered food secure?

options.A: When they have a large bank balance
options.B: When enough food is available for all, and all have the capacity to buy food of acceptable quality
options.C: When they live near a farm
options.D: When they eat only expensive food

questions.explanation: Food security is ensured only if there is enough food, it's accessible, and there are no barriers to obtaining it.

questions.question: The most devastating famine that occurred in India in 1943 was:
questions.explanation: The Famine of Bengal in 1943 killed 30 lakh people and remains the most devastating famine in Indian history.

questions.question: Which group of people is most prone to food insecurity in rural areas?
questions.explanation: Landless people, traditional artisans, and petty self-employed workers are the most vulnerable in rural sectors.

questions.question: Chronic hunger is a consequence of:

options.B: Diets persistently inadequate in terms of quantity and/or quality

questions.explanation: Chronic hunger occurs when people cannot afford or access a nutritionally balanced diet consistently.

questions.question: Seasonal hunger is mostly found in:

options.B: Rural areas due to the seasonal nature of agriculture

questions.explanation: It is related to cycles of food growing and harvesting where work is not available throughout the year.

questions.question: The 'Green Revolution' in India was primarily associated with the production of:
questions.explanation: The Green Revolution introduced HYV seeds which massively increased wheat and rice yields.

questions.question: Which organization procures foodgrains like wheat and rice for the government?
questions.explanation: The FCI is responsible for purchasing, storing, and distributing foodgrains in India.

questions.question: What is the price pre-announced by the government to support farmers called?
questions.explanation: MSP is announced before the sowing season to provide incentives and security to farmers.

questions.question: The price at which grains are distributed through ration shops to the poor is:
questions.explanation: The Issue Price is lower than the market price to make food affordable for the poorer sections.

questions.question: Ration shops are also known as:
questions.explanation: They are called Fair Price Shops because they sell essential goods at subsidized, government-regulated rates.

questions.question: Which ration card is issued to the 'poorest of the poor'?
questions.explanation: Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) specifically targets the bottom-most stratum of the poor.

questions.question: The National Food Security Act was passed in the year:
questions.explanation: The NFSA 2013 provides a legal right to subsidized foodgrains for a large portion of India's population.

questions.question: FCI distributes foodgrains through a network of:
questions.explanation: There are about 5.5 lakh ration shops all over the country forming the PDS network.

questions.question: In which state are 94% of fair price shops run by cooperatives?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu is a leading example where cooperatives effectively manage the Public Distribution System.

questions.question: Mother Dairy provides milk and vegetables at controlled rates in:
questions.explanation: Mother Dairy is making strides in providing essential products at government-decided rates in Delhi.

questions.question: The 'White Revolution' in India is related to the production of:
questions.explanation: Amul is the success story behind the White Revolution, which made India the largest milk producer.

questions.question: Academy of Development Science (ADS) has facilitated setting up of _________ in Maharashtra.
questions.explanation: ADS facilitated a network of NGOs to set up grain banks for tribal communities in Maharashtra.

questions.question: Under the AAY scheme, 35 kg of foodgrains are provided at what price for wheat?
questions.explanation: Antyodaya Anna Yojana provides wheat at ₹2 per kg and rice at ₹3 per kg.

questions.question: Which age group is specifically targeted under the ICDS scheme for primary health and nutrition?
questions.explanation: Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) focuses on the nutritional needs of infants and pregnant/nursing mothers.

questions.question: What is the result of a natural calamity like drought on food security?

options.C: Decline in production leads to shortage and price rise

questions.explanation: Shortage of food in an area during a calamity makes food unaffordable for many.

questions.question: What is the buffer stock norm?

options.A: The minimum quantity of grain the government must maintain
options.B: The total production of a year

questions.explanation: The government maintains a specific norm of foodgrain stocks to handle emergencies and distribution.

questions.question: Which of these is a mal-practice by PDS dealers?

options.B: Diverting grains to open market and selling poor quality grain

questions.explanation: Diversion of grains to the open market for profit is a major challenge in the PDS system.

questions.question: Which state became a model for the Green Revolution due to its wheat production?
questions.explanation: Punjab and Haryana were the biggest beneficiaries and success stories of the Green Revolution.

questions.question: Food security means availability, accessibility, and _________.
questions.explanation: Affordability is the third pillar, ensuring individuals have the money to buy food.
